title: ABCDE APPROACH
content: Initial evaluation of unstable patients follows ABCDE sequence. Airway. Breathing. Circulation. Disability. Exposure.

quiz.q: What is the first step when assessing an unstable patient?

title: AIRWAY OBSTRUCTION
content: Signs include inability to speak, stridor, cyanosis. Immediate airway management required.

quiz.q: What is the most urgent step in airway obstruction?

title: MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION
content: Typical presentation includes chest pressure, radiation to arm or jaw, sweating and nausea.

quiz.q: What is the first diagnostic test in suspected MI?

title: STEMI MANAGEMENT
content: ST elevation myocardial infarction requires urgent reperfusion therapy.

quiz.q: What is the definitive treatment for STEMI?

title: PNEUMOTHORAX
content: Air in pleural space causing lung collapse. Symptoms include sudden dyspnea and chest pain.

quiz.q: What physical finding suggests pneumothorax?

title: TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX
content: Life threatening condition with tracheal deviation, hypotension, severe dyspnea.

quiz.q: What is the immediate treatment?

title: STROKE
content: Sudden neurological deficit such as weakness, speech difficulty, vision loss.

quiz.q: What imaging is first used in stroke?

title: INTRACRANIAL HEMORRHAGE
content: Bleeding inside the skull causing increased intracranial pressure.

quiz.q: What symptom strongly suggests intracranial bleeding?

title: ASTHMA EXACERBATION
content: Acute worsening of asthma with wheezing and shortness of breath.

quiz.q: What medication relieves acute asthma symptoms?

title: COPD
content: Chronic airflow limitation usually due to smoking.

quiz.q: What is the main risk factor for COPD?

title: UPPER GI BLEED
content: Bleeding from esophagus stomach or duodenum.

quiz.q: What symptom suggests upper GI bleeding?

title: PEPTIC ULCER
content: Ulceration of stomach or duodenum often caused by H pylori or NSAIDs.

quiz.q: What bacterium causes many peptic ulcers?

title: ACUTE KIDNEY INJURY
content: Sudden decline in kidney function leading to rise in creatinine.

quiz.q: What lab marker rises in acute kidney injury?

title: URINARY TRACT INFECTION
content: Bacterial infection of urinary tract with dysuria and frequency.

quiz.q: What symptom strongly suggests UTI?

title: DIABETES MELLITUS
content: Chronic hyperglycemia due to insulin deficiency or resistance.

quiz.q: What hormone regulates blood glucose?

title: HYPERTHYROIDISM
content: Excess thyroid hormone causing weight loss heat intolerance and tachycardia.

quiz.q: What symptom suggests hyperthyroidism?

title: SEPSIS
content: Systemic inflammatory response due to infection causing organ dysfunction.

quiz.q: What treatment should be given early in sepsis?

title: ANEMIA
content: Reduction in hemoglobin leading to fatigue pallor and shortness of breath.

quiz.q: What symptom commonly occurs in anemia?

title: DEPRESSION
content: Persistent low mood loss of interest sleep and appetite changes.

quiz.q: What term describes loss of pleasure?

title: ANAPHYLAXIS
content: Severe allergic reaction with airway swelling hypotension and rash.

quiz.q: What is the first line treatment for anaphylaxis?
